The OD Number is an identifier for a specific determination event or outcome. An Observation resource can represent the outcome of a determination, and its identifier field can store this number.
A contract number is an identifier for an organization (e.g., a health plan), fitting the Organization.identifier field.
A Plan Benefit Package is an identifier for a specific offering by an organization, fitting the Organization.identifier field.
The Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) is a key identifier for a patient, explicitly listed in the Patient resource's CMS concepts.

This concept describes the *type* or *role* of the party requesting the determination (e.g., enrollee, provider, plan). While the actual party would be a Patient, Practitioner, or Organization, the *role* itself can be captured as a coded value within an Observation representing the determination.
The National Provider Identifier (NPI) is a standard identifier for practitioners, explicitly listed in the Practitioner resource's common fields.

concept: Was this a contracted provider referral? (Y/N)
reasoning: This is a boolean flag indicating a specific condition related to the determination. An Observation can capture such a boolean value, with a specific code defining the concept.

concept: Item/Service/Part B Drug Code (CPT, HCPCS, J code, DRG) - CPT, HCPCS
reasoning: CPT and HCPCS codes are standard for identifying procedures and services, directly mapping to Procedure.code.

concept: Item/Service/Part B Drug Code (CPT, HCPCS, J code, DRG) - J code
reasoning: J codes are used for Part B drugs, which are medications. MedicationRequest.medicationCodeableConcept is the appropriate field for medication codes.

concept: Item/Service/Part B Drug Code (CPT, HCPCS, J code, DRG) - DRG
reasoning: DRG (Diagnosis Related Group) is a classification for an entire hospital stay (Encounter) based on diagnoses and procedures, rather than a single item/service code. Encounter.type can classify the type of encounter, which is the closest fit in the catalog.

concept: Item/Service/Part B Drug Description - for services
reasoning: The description for a service or item is typically the display text associated with its code, found in Procedure.code.text.

concept: Item/Service/Part B Drug Description - for drugs
reasoning: The description for a Part B drug is typically the display text associated with its code, found in MedicationRequest.medicationCodeableConcept.text.
ICD-10 codes are standard for diagnoses, directly mapping to Condition.code. HIPPS codes are often derived from diagnoses.
This is a boolean flag indicating a specific condition related to the determination. An Observation can capture such a boolean value, with a specific code defining the concept.
This is a coded value describing the priority of the determination process. An Observation can capture such a coded value, with a specific code defining the concept.
This is a boolean flag indicating a specific condition related to the determination. An Observation can capture such a boolean value, with a specific code defining the concept.
This is a timestamp for an event in the determination process. An Observation can capture the effective date/time of an event it represents.
This is a timestamp for an event in the determination process. An Observation can capture the effective date/time of an event it represents.

concept: Disposition (Fully favorable, Partially favorable, Adverse, Dismissal)
reasoning: This is the core outcome of the determination, a coded value that can be captured by Observation.valueCodeableConcept.
This is a specific coded reason for a dismissal, which can be captured by Observation.valueCodeableConcept.
This is a specific coded reason for a decision, which can be captured by Observation.valueCodeableConcept. The organizational decision affects the value's format, not the FHIR field.

concept: Reviewer Qualifications (Physician, Other Appropriate Healthcare Professional)
reasoning: This describes the professional qualifications of an individual practitioner, directly mapping to Practitioner.qualification.

concept: Were internal plan coverage criteria applied? (Y/N)
reasoning: This is a boolean flag indicating a specific condition related to the determination. An Observation can capture such a boolean value, with a specific code defining the concept.

concept: Did a third-party vendor participate, in any capacity, in the determination review or decision-making? (Y/N)
reasoning: This is a boolean flag indicating a specific condition related to the determination. An Observation can capture such a boolean value, with a specific code defining the concept.

concept: For partially or fully favorable decisions, was the approved item, service or Part B drug different from what was requested? (Y/N)
reasoning: This is a boolean flag indicating a specific condition related to the determination outcome. An Observation can capture such a boolean value, with a specific code defining the concept.

concept: Procedure code for the approved item, service, or Part B drug (if different from requested) - for services
reasoning: This refers to the code for an approved procedure or service, directly mapping to Procedure.code.

concept: Procedure code for the approved item, service, or Part B drug (if different from requested) - for drugs
reasoning: This refers to the code for an approved Part B drug, directly mapping to MedicationRequest.medicationCodeableConcept.
The zip code of a service location is part of the address of a Location resource, which can be referenced from an Encounter or Procedure.
Place of Service codes classify the setting of an encounter. Encounter.class provides broad categories like inpatient, outpatient, emergency, which align with this concept.
The start date of a service is captured by the period.start field of an Encounter or Procedure.
The end date of a service is captured by the period.end field of an Encounter or Procedure.
This is a timestamp for when a claim was received. An Observation can capture the effective date/time of an event it represents, such as a claim receipt.
This is a boolean flag about the claim. An Observation can capture such a boolean value, with a specific code defining the concept.

concept: Was prior approval (e.g., a prior authorization or voluntary pre-service request) requested? (Y/N)
reasoning: This is a boolean flag indicating whether prior approval was requested. An Observation can capture such a boolean value, with a specific code defining the concept.

concept: OD number for associated prior approval request (if prior approval requested)
reasoning: This is an identifier for a prior approval request. An Observation can represent the prior approval event/outcome, and its identifier field can store this number.

concept: Was prior authorization a required condition for coverage? (Y/N)
reasoning: This is a boolean flag indicating a condition for coverage. An Observation can capture such a boolean value, with a specific code defining the concept.
The Appeal Number is an identifier for a specific appeal determination event or outcome. An Observation can represent the outcome of an appeal, and its identifier field can store this number.

concept: Is this an appeal of an OD dismissal? (Y/N)
reasoning: This is a boolean flag indicating a specific condition related to the appeal. An Observation can capture such a boolean value, with a specific code defining the concept.

concept: Was the initial OD request denied for lack of medical necessity? (Y/N)
reasoning: This is a boolean flag indicating a specific condition related to the initial determination. An Observation can capture such a boolean value, with a specific code defining the concept.

concept: Was the reconsideration request reviewed by a physician? (Y/N)
reasoning: This is a boolean flag indicating a specific condition related to the reconsideration review. An Observation can capture such a boolean value, with a specific code defining the concept.
This defines the time frame for which data is reported. While not a direct FHIR field, it represents a filter applied to date/time fields like Observation.effectiveDateTime or Encounter.period.
These are specific types of organizations, directly mapping to the Organization.type field which describes the kind of organization.
This indicates the death status of a patient, directly mapping to Patient.deceased[x].
This refers to the outcome of a hospital stay, directly mapping to Encounter.hospitalization.dischargeDisposition.
These are specific types of organizations, directly mapping to the Organization.type field which describes the kind of organization.
These are specific types of organizations, directly mapping to the Organization.type field which describes the kind of organization.
These are specific types of organizations, directly mapping to the Organization.type field which describes the kind of organization.
An Independent Review Entity (IRE) is a type of organization, directly mapping to Organization.type.
An Administrative Law Judge is a type of professional qualification for a practitioner. Could also refer to an Organization if referring to the office.
An Appeals Council is a type of organization, directly mapping to Organization.type.
This is a general reference to a healthcare service or item, which is broadly represented by the Procedure resource.
This is a general reference to a healthcare provider or supplier, which is broadly represented by the Practitioner resource (or Organization for an entity).
This is a general reference to medications, specifically Part B drugs, which are broadly represented by the MedicationRequest resource.
A Quality Improvement Organization (QIO) is a type of organization, directly mapping to Organization.type.
This is a general reference to healthcare services, which are broadly represented by the Procedure resource.
A Skilled Nursing Facility (SNF) stay is a type of inpatient encounter, directly mapping to Encounter.class.
This is a general reference to a healthcare service, which is broadly represented by the Procedure resource.
This is a general reference to a health plan, which is broadly represented by the Organization resource.
This is an identifier for a related determination. The Observation resource is used to represent the determination outcome, and its identifier field can hold this associated number.
This concept describes the specific type of determination being reported. An Observation can represent the determination event, and its code field can specify whether it's an 'Initial Determination'.
This concept describes the specific type of determination being reported. An Observation can represent the determination event, and its code field can specify whether it's a 'Reconsideration'.
This indicates the completion status of the determination process, which directly maps to the status field of an Observation resource representing the determination.
A 'substantive decision' implies that a final outcome has been reached for the determination. This aligns with the status field of an Observation (e.g., 'final', 'completed').

concept: Denial of a Medicare request for coverage
reasoning: This describes a specific outcome of a determination, which is a type of Disposition (e.g., 'Adverse'). This maps to Observation.valueCodeableConcept where the code represents the denial.

This refers to a type of payment or coverage. While not directly supported by a specific FHIR field in the catalog, an Observation can capture this as a coded value, indicating the source or type of payment/provision.
This describes the type of criteria applied during a determination. An Observation can represent the application of criteria, and its code field can specify the type (e.g., Medicare, Medicaid).

concept: Denials based on exhaustion of Medicare benefits
reasoning: This is a specific reason for a denial, which falls under the 'Decision Rationale' concept already mapped to Observation.valueCodeableConcept.
This describes the patient's destination after an inpatient stay, which is captured by Encounter.hospitalization.dischargeDisposition.
This refers to a specific category of services. The Procedure resource represents services, and its code field can specify the type of service, including 'supplemental'.
This describes the nature of a favorable decision. An Observation can capture the outcome of a determination, and its valueCodeableConcept can represent this specific type of decision.

This concept describes that a single determination can cover multiple services/items. While not a direct field, an Observation representing the determination could link to multiple Procedure or MedicationRequest resources via 'related' to represent these line items.
A 'physician's office' is a type of healthcare organization, directly mapping to Organization.type.
This describes the contractual relationship of a provider. An Observation can capture this as a coded value, with a subject reference to the Practitioner or Organization.
This refers to a decision about a service. An Observation can represent the determination, and its code can specify it as a 'service decision'.
This describes the automated processing method for a claim. An Observation can capture this as a coded value, related to the claim itself.
This refers to a specific type of authorization decision. An Observation can represent this determination, and its code can specify it as 'concurrent authorization'.

concept: requests to continue previously authorized ongoing courses of treatment
reasoning: This describes a specific type of request for a determination. An Observation can represent the determination event, and its code can specify this type of request.

This refers to a planned series of interventions or therapies. While a single Procedure represents an action, a 'course of treatment' implies multiple, which could be represented by a high-level Procedure code or a series of Procedure resources.
This describes a specific outcome of a determination regarding services. It's a type of Disposition or Decision Rationale, mapping to Observation.valueCodeableConcept.
This describes the timing/nature of a request for a determination. An Observation can represent the determination event, and its code can specify it as a 'pre-service request'.
This describes a specific type of request for a determination. An Observation can represent the determination event, and its code can specify it as a 'claims payment request'.
This refers to an administrative process to re-evaluate a prior decision. An Observation can represent this event, and its code can specify it as a 'reopening'.
This describes a specific type of request. An Observation can represent the request event, and its code can specify it as a 'duplicate payment request'.
This describes a general type of request for payment. An Observation can represent the request event, and its code can specify it as a 'payment request'.
This describes a specific outcome for a claim. An Observation can represent the claim processing outcome, and its valueCodeableConcept can represent a 'rejection'.
This is a general term encompassing both claims and requests for determination. An Observation can represent either, with its code specifying the type.
This refers to a type of healthcare benefit. While not directly supported by a specific FHIR field in the catalog, an Observation can capture this as a coded value, indicating the type of benefit.

concept: Medicaid home-and community-based long-term services and supports
reasoning: This describes a specific category of services. The Procedure resource represents services, and its code field can specify the type of service.

concept: Plan decisions regarding a request to vacate a dismissal
reasoning: This describes a specific type of decision made by a plan. An Observation can represent this determination, and its code can specify it as a 'decision to vacate a dismissal'.
Approved mapping. These are specific types of organizations, directly mapping to the Organization.type field which describes the kind of organization.

concept: State Medicaid and Children’s Health Insurance Program (CHIP) fee-for-service (FFS) programs
reasoning: These represent types of governmental or payer organizations responsible for FFS programs, fitting Organization.type.
These are specific types of health plans/organizations, directly mapping to Organization.type.
These are specific types of health plans/organizations, directly mapping to Organization.type.

concept: Qualified Health Plan (QHP) issuers on the Federally Facilitated Exchanges (FFEs)
reasoning: These are specific types of health plans/organizations, directly mapping to Organization.type.
Approved mapping for 'service or item' is Procedure. This resource represents actions performed on or for a patient for diagnostic or therapeutic purposes.

A prior authorization event or its outcome can be represented by an Observation. The 'code' field would specify that this Observation pertains to a 'prior authorization' request or decision. This aligns with how 'Initial Determinations' and 'Reconsiderations' are mapped to Observation.code.
This is a specific type/priority of a prior authorization request. The approved mapping for 'Processing Priority (Standard, Expedited)' uses Observation.valueCodeableConcept.
This is a specific type/priority of a prior authorization request. The approved mapping for 'Processing Priority (Standard, Expedited)' uses Observation.valueCodeableConcept.

'Approved' refers to the outcome of a prior authorization request. This maps to the 'Disposition' concept, which is approved to Observation.valueCodeableConcept (e.g., 'Fully favorable', 'Partially favorable'). The percentage itself is a derived metric, not a direct FHIR field.

'Denied' refers to the outcome of a prior authorization request. This maps to the 'Disposition' concept, which is approved to Observation.valueCodeableConcept (e.g., 'Adverse'). The percentage itself is a derived metric, not a direct FHIR field.

An appeal is a subsequent event related to an initial prior authorization decision. While 'Appeal Number' maps to Observation.identifier, the *relationship* of a decision being 'after appeal' implies linking a new Observation (the appeal decision) to a previous Observation (the initial decision) using Observation.related.

Response time is calculated from two timestamps: 'Date Request Received' and 'Date of Decision', both approved to Observation.effectiveDateTime. The duration itself is a calculated metric, but these fields provide the necessary data points.

This is a boolean condition related to the prior authorization process. An Observation can capture such a boolean value, with a specific code defining the concept. Similar to 'Was prior authorization required? (Y/N)'.
This is a time dimension for reporting. 'Report Period' is approved to Observation.effectiveDateTime. This field would be used to filter or group prior authorization Observation resources by year.

This refers to a specific business segment or contract level of an organization (e.g., MA contract level, plan level). This information is typically captured as an identifier or a type attribute of the Organization. Organization.identifier is a good fit for contract or plan IDs.
A payer is a type of organization, explicitly listed in Organization's CMS concepts.

A provider is a type of practitioner, explicitly listed in Practitioner's CMS concepts. Could also be Organization if referring to a provider organization, but Practitioner is more common for the requesting party.
Approved mapping for 'Part D drugs' and 'Part B drugs' is MedicationRequest. This concept refers to the resource type that would be excluded.

This refers to the count of all prior authorization requests. Each request would be represented by an Observation (as per 'prior authorization' mapping). The count is a derived metric, but the underlying data points are Observation resources.

This refers to the count of all prior authorization appeals. Each appeal decision would be represented by an Observation (as per 'after appeal' mapping). The count is a derived metric, but the underlying data points are Observation resources.

concept: medical items and services that require prior authorization
reasoning: This refers to specific Procedure codes. The 'requirement' for prior authorization is a business rule associated with these procedures, which could be represented by an Observation linking to the Procedure or by metadata associated with the Procedure itself. Focusing on the 'items and services' part, Procedure.code is the most direct.
